input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to:
1. Check what .NET versions are available
2. Create a benchmark project that targets both .NET 9 and .NET 10
3. Run it with BenchmarkDotNet

Let me check available .NET SDKs first.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet --list-sdks && echo \"---\" && dotnet --list-runtimes", "description": "Check available .NET SDKs and runtimes", "initial_wait": 10}
